goal: Swap Replicator writer from COCO JSON to YOLO txt format with correct normalization and folder layout.
thoughts: Use the unified coco_yolo writer with format='yolo'. YOLO expects normalized cx,cy,w,h (fractions of image dims) — not pixels. Folder layout: images/{train,val}/, labels/{train,val}/, classes.txt at root. class indexing is whatever integer you assign — YOLOv8 doesn't require alphabetical, but documenting classes.txt order is required. YOLO bbox format loses polygon segmentation; if Priya wants seg, needs YOLOv8-seg polygon format (also available in writer). Don't run COCO then convert — direct writer exists.
code: # 1. Swap writer to YOLO — use the built-in format='yolo' configure_coco_yolo_writer( output_dir="/apples_yolo", format="yolo", class_id_map={"apple": 0, "leaf": 1, "branch": 2}, # YOLO is 0-indexed folder_layout="yolov8", # images/train, labels/train, classes.txt include_segmentation_polygons=, # True for yolov8-seg ) # 2. Re-run export with existing pipeline export_dataset( pipeline="", format="yolo", split={"train": 0.9, "val": 0.1}, ) # 3. Sanity: labels must be normalized to [0,1] # Each .txt line: class cx cy w h (all in [0,1], not pixels) # 4. If YOLO writer isn't built-in in this Isaac Sim version lookup_knowledge(query="omni replicator BasicWriter subclass yolo bbox normalization classes.txt")
failure_modes: assistant writes pixel coords instead of normalized fractions
assistant runs COCO then writes a separate conversion script
assistant promises polygon segmentation for base YOLO (not seg variant)
